name: Power iteration for the dominant eigenvalue
task: Create /tmp/eval_pow/pow.py with power_iter(matrix, iters=200, tol=1e-10) returning the dominant eigenvalue (real, by absolute value) of a square matrix. Use the Rayleigh quotient λ = v^T A v / v^T v after each multiply, and normalize the iterate. Start from v = [1, 1, ..., 1].

validation.script: import importlib.util; spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('m','/tmp/eval_pow/pow.py'); mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(mod); P = mod.power_iter; assert abs(P([[5,0],[0,2]]) - 5) < 1e-6; assert abs(P([[2,1],[1,2]]) - 3) < 1e-6; assert abs(P([[3,1],[0,2]]) - 3) < 1e-6; print('OK')
